pi_items.source: PMC3849296: Systematic review of viral gastroenteritis incubation periods found Norovirus median 29 hours (1.2 days)
CDC Yellow Book and PMC3849296: Norovirus incubation period range is 12-48 hours; median ~33.5 hours
PMC8845055: Association of host, agent and environment characteristics study found Norovirus median incubation period 33.5 hours (95% CI 32.0-34.0)
Comparison of pathogen incubation periods: Salmonella ~45 hours (contradicts), Staphylococcus aureus 4-10 hours (contradicts), Norovirus 29-34 hours (matches)
Incubation period of ~33 hours is pathognomonic for Norovirus among common gastroenteritis pathogens. PMC8845055

StigenOutbreak not documented in accessible public health databases. Dataset appears to be fictional/synthetic.

privileged_info_summary: The ground truth incubation period median of 33.0 hours is verified against published epidemiological research (PMC8845055), which reports 33.5 hours (95% CI 32.0-34.0) for Norovirus gastroenteritis. The expected median range of 28-40 hours is consistent with CDC guidelines (12-48 hour range for Norovirus). Norovirus is the correct pathogen identification for a 33-hour median incubation period, as Salmonella typically has ~45 hour median and Staphylococcus aureus has 4-10 hour median. The specific 'StigenOutbreak' dataset with 91 cases could not be verified in public outbreak databases and appears to be a synthetic or proprietary dataset. Task infrastructure issues: Referenced setup_task.ps1 and export_result.ps1 scripts do not exist; only export_result.sh is present.
